sourceTitle: Your ultimate guide to building a personal wiki in Notion

questions.question: What is the recommended first step when building a personal wiki in Notion?

options.text: Create a top‑level page to serve as a home base
options.explanation: The guide says to start with a top‑level page as a home base and then add sections like Notes, Work, Life, and Planning.

options.text: Immediately add a database of tasks
options.explanation: While databases are useful, the guide does not list them as the first step.

options.text: Install a third‑party wiki plugin
options.explanation: Notion’s built‑in pages are sufficient; the guide never mentions any external plugin.

options.text: Share the wiki with the entire team before adding any content
options.explanation: Sharing is optional and comes later; the guide focuses on structuring the wiki first.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a way to customize a personal wiki page?

options.text: Adding a page icon
options.explanation: Page icons are specifically mentioned as a customization option.

options.text: Changing the font color
options.explanation: Font colors are included in the guide’s list of customizations.

options.text: Embedding a live video stream
options.explanation: Embedding a live video stream is not mentioned; the guide only talks about icons, cover images, quotes, and font colors.

options.text: Setting a cover image
options.explanation: Cover images are explicitly listed as a customization feature.

questions.question: How can you quickly create subpages within a Notion personal wiki?

options.text: Use slash commands to generate a new page block
options.explanation: The guide advises using slash commands to create subpages, which is the fastest method.

options.text: Copy‑paste an existing page from another workspace
options.explanation: Copy‑pasting works but isn’t the method highlighted for quick subpage creation.

options.text: Drag a file from your computer into the page
options.explanation: Dragging a file creates an attachment, not a subpage.

options.text: Click the "New Template" button at the bottom of the sidebar
options.explanation: Templates are useful for starting new pages but aren’t the primary way to add subpages on the fly.

questions.question: What advantage does nesting pages inside other pages provide?

options.text: It enables infinite organization possibilities
options.explanation: The article states that nesting pages allows for infinite organization possibilities.

options.text: It automatically syncs the page with external cloud services
options.explanation: Nesting does not affect external syncing; Notion handles syncing uniformly.

options.text: It locks the page so no one else can edit it
options.explanation: Nesting does not change permission settings.

options.text: It reduces the file size of the workspace
options.explanation: Page nesting has no impact on file size.

questions.question: Where can you find pre‑made templates to jump‑start your personal wiki?

options.text: In Notion’s template gallery, which hosts over 10,000 templates
options.explanation: The guide explicitly points readers to the template gallery with more than 10,000 templates.

options.text: On the Notion YouTube channel’s description box
options.explanation: The YouTube channel is mentioned for videos, not for template downloads.

options.text: Within the sidebar under "Favorites"
options.explanation: Favorites are user‑selected pages, not a source of new templates.

options.text: By contacting Notion support via chat
options.explanation: Support can help with questions, but the guide directs users to the template gallery for templates.

questions.question: Which feature of Notion allows you to view and navigate all of your wiki pages efficiently?

options.text: The sidebar, which lists pages and lets you nest them
options.explanation: The article notes that you can access pages from the sidebar and nest pages inside pages, providing efficient navigation.

options.text: The global search bar at the top of the screen
options.explanation: While the search bar is useful, the guide emphasizes the sidebar for organization and navigation.

options.text: The recent activity feed on the home page
options.explanation: The activity feed shows changes but isn’t described as the primary navigation tool for a personal wiki.

options.text: The mobile app’s swipe‑gesture menu
options.explanation: The mobile swipe menu isn’t mentioned in the guide; the sidebar is the highlighted feature.

questions.question: How can you organize sections like Notes, Work, Life, and Planning within your personal wiki?

options.text: Create subpages for each section and arrange them in columns
options.explanation: The guide recommends using slash commands to create subpages and then organizing them into columns.

options.text: Add each section as a separate database and link them together
options.explanation: Databases are powerful but the guide focuses on simple subpages and columns, not databases.

options.text: Insert a table of contents block at the top of the page
options.explanation: A table of contents helps navigation but doesn’t create the structural sections described.

options.text: Use emojis in the page title to differentiate sections
options.explanation: Emojis can be decorative, but the guide does not list them as the main method for organizing sections.
